channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kaye Abad shares her journey with breast cancer and celebrates recovery
item.description: Kaye Abad, a well-known Filipino actress, recently opened up about her personal health journey, revealing that she was diagnosed with breast cancer. In her heartfelt announcement, she expressed relief...

item.title: APC Releases Official Winners of Adamawa House of Representatives Primaries
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don officially announce the winners of their House of Representatives primaries for all eight federal constituencies inside Adamawa State. Ahmed Yusha’u wey be chai...

item.title: Keith and Amanda Discuss Politics, Monarchy, and Society Over Dinner
item.description: Keith, a 48-year-old medical microbiology lecturer from Leeds, and Amanda, a 67-year-old family lawyer from Doncaster, participated in a dinner designed to bridge political and cultural divides. Despi...

item.title: The Supremes: Elegance and Innovation on Stage in the 1960s
item.description: Watching The Supremes perform in the 1960s was a display of elegance, precision, and style rather than high-energy spectacle. Under Motown founder Berry Gordy's direction, Diana Ross, Mary Wilson, and...

item.title: Consolidated Bank of Kenya Seeks Qualified Kenyans for Multiple ICT, Banking and Managerial Positions
item.description: Consolidated Bank of Kenya has opened up several job opportunities across different departments, targeting qualified Kenyan professionals. The bank is inviting applications for twelve positions includ...

item.title: Siyakholwa Kuse to Make First WBC Title Defence in South Africa
item.description: Siyakholwa Kuse has made history by capturing the WBC minimumweight title, defeating Filipino boxer Melvin Jerusalem via unanimous decision at Emperors Palace. His promoter, Rodney Berman, confirmed t...

item.title: Bishop Bacani urges senators to prioritize public duty over personal loyalty in Sara Duterte impeachment
item.description: A day before the Senate could convene as an impeachment court for Vice President Sara Duterte, Novaliches Bishop Teodoro Bacani reminded senators that their primary duty is to serve the Filipino peopl...

item.title: US Central Command Recognises Pakistan’s Key Role in Countering IS-K Terrorism
item.description: The US Central Command (Centcom) has officially recognised Pakistan as a vital partner in the fight against the Islamic State-Khorasan (IS-K) in South Asia. Navy Admiral Bradford Cooper, addressing th...

item.title: Nigeria Approves Smart Transport Data Bank to Boost National Transport System
item.description: The Federal Executive Council (FEC) don give green light to the Smart National Transport Databank (SNTDB) Project wey the Nigerian Institute of Transport Technology (NITT) dey run under the Federal Mi...

item.title: Toronto man fatally stabbed in Etobicoke apartment overnight
item.description: Toronto police are investigating a fatal stabbing that occurred overnight in an apartment building in Etobicoke. The victim, a man, was found with severe injuries and later pronounced dead at the scen...

item.title: Caritas Programs Help Ruvuma Residents Improve Livelihoods and Reduce Poverty
item.description: Communities in Ruvuma Region have been encouraged to take full advantage of economic empowerment programmes offered by Caritas, the Catholic Church's charity, to enhance livelihoods and combat poverty...

item.title: Government to build 287 communication towers to connect rural Tanzania
item.description: The Tanzanian government is taking major steps to reduce the digital gap between urban and rural areas by investing Sh29 billion to build 287 communication towers in underserved regions. The project, ...

item.title: Lisa Nandy comments on Keir Starmer and potential Labour leadership contest
item.description: Culture Secretary Lisa Nandy has emphasised that it is a personal decision for Labour leader Sir Keir Starmer whether he chooses to enter any potential leadership contest. Speaking on the BBC's Sunday...

item.title: The Rising Dangers Faced by Journalists Around the World
item.description: Journalism today faces unprecedented dangers, with reporters increasingly targeted in conflict zones and politically volatile regions. In 2025, 129 journalists and media workers were killed, the highe...

item.title: Proposed High-Voltage Transmission Line Sparks Debate Over Anza-Borrego Desert State Park
item.description: San Diego Gas & Electric has proposed constructing a 140-mile, 500-kilovolt transmission line, named the Golden Pacific Powerlink, that would cut through Anza-Borrego Desert State Park, California's l...

item.title: Bangladesh Take Strong Lead in Second Test Against Pakistan
item.description: In the second Test between Bangladesh and Pakistan in Sylhet, Bangladesh has taken a strong position after day two. Opener Mahmudul Hasan Joy made a fine comeback from a first-innings duck, scoring 52...

item.title: Highway 427 Off-Ramp to Rutherford Road Temporarily Closed Following Overnight Collision in Vaughan
item.description: Authorities have temporarily closed the off-ramp from Highway 427 to Rutherford Road in Vaughan following an overnight crash. The incident occurred during the early hours of May 17, 2026, and prompted...

item.title: Tamil Nadu Fishermen Appeal to Centre to Reduce Fuel Prices Amid Rising Costs
item.description: The Tamil Nadu Fishermen Federation has formally appealed to the Union government to reconsider the recent rise in petrol and diesel prices, highlighting the severe impact it could have on the livelih...

item.title: UAE-Kenya Partnership Boosts Trade, Infrastructure, and Industrial Growth in East Africa
item.description: For a long time, Kenya’s trade with global partners has largely been defined by aid dependency and extractive relationships, leaving small-scale farmers and young job-seekers with limited benefits. Ho...

item.title: The 1951 Allard J2: Iconic British-American Sports Car
item.description: The 1951 Allard J2 is a legendary British sports car created by Sydney Allard, combining lightweight British engineering with powerful American V8 engines from Cadillac or Chrysler. Known for its raw,...
